title: pigafetta filaris
intro: Pigafetta filaris, also known as the New Guinea yam or mountain yam, is a species of flowering plant in the family Dioscoreaceae. Native to the tropical highlands of New Guinea, this plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The roots and tubers of Pigafetta filaris contain compounds that have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing blood pressure and cholesterol levels, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of Pigafetta filaris are often made from the roots and tubers, which can be dried and used as a tea or powder, or cooked and eaten as a vegetable.
